reviews.review_msg: Dr. K is tough and strict but this is for your own benefit. Her leading her classes in this way ensure you actually stay on top of things because the course is content dense. She's also extremely kind, compassionate and approachable. I think she is probably the best prof in the MBB department. Put in the work, as you should! She's great and fair!!
Dr. K is the analogy queen. She makes you understand.
Do not take, wait for another teacher to teach the class. If not, look for other class alternatives.

Honestly, if you get your money's worth from tuition and actually attend her lectures, she'll tend to *really* emphasize certain things that are examinable. There is a LOT of material in this course, so I can kinda see why she can sometimes seem rushed, but she was very approachable after lecture and really good at clarifying things I didn't get

I had Dr K for 229 and 222. She's the best prof i have had in MBB. Go to lectures and go to office hours. She can seem intimidating at first but she's so knowledgable in her field and soooo helpful if you just go and ask. Can not wait for 309 with her. Take her courses!
She is the best prof I've had in mbb. Go to office hours. Tells you whats on exams if you go to lecture. Take her courses!

Tbh she's not bad. Just really have to memorize every key detail of each lecture. Exams make are entirely your grade so do your best on the first two midterms. Final exam is not culumative even tho she says it is! Focus on the last few lectures and you should be ok.

Lecture slides are very text heavy yet do not have most of the important information on them (she will make additional notes on slides which you will not have access to). She does not record lectures or let you have your phone out to record lectures. So if you miss a day, you miss all the important information. Exams are VERY specific.
such boring lectures.

MBB courses are content heavy no matter what. I think some people just did not want to put in the work and that's why they did not like this class. Dr. K was fairly clear in explanations and about what was important, however, her lecture slides were crammed with information towards the end. Textbook was recommended but I did not use it much.

I enjoyed 222. Just need to go into the class with the understanding that it is a really heavy course. Really dense, detail-oriented, and full course that takes consistent studying. Put in the work and youll do well. I found her lecture notes to be really useful, only used the textbook a handful of times when I needed clarification. Good office hrs

Her slides were a mix of pictures with some text heavy slides like paragraphs and would just tell us to read it ourselves. She moves at a very fast pace. I mostly self taught myself with youtube videos because a lot of what she said and in the slides were just not detailed enough to understand. She explains it like you know the topics prior.

Awesome proff IF you put in the work. Its a dense course. Go to lecture she tells you what is on the exams. Super helpful in office hours. Don't expect to do well if you dont go to class. Would take again.

I thought that Irinia was decent.. She grades pretty difficult and is specific with her answers. With that said, I give her the benefit of the doubt. This is a VERY dense course with a lot to go over. You dont have the time to coast nor assume things are gonna be easy lol because they wont be. She gave me a good grade so I cant complain much though
Avoid at all cost! garbage prof! Boring and heavy lecture and she does not give a F about the students.

This prof says cumulative final then makes it not cumulative and makes readings required when the textbook is very expensive but the free version we find has very bad formatting. tutorials are mandatory and i had bad experience with the TAs as well. her lecture is very boring and hard to focus on, you can catch many people sleeping.

Irina's a good prof. Not bad but not great, she does her job and helps students do it decently well. MBB222 is a really dense and heavy course, so there's, unfortunately, no way around that. Lectures are really heavy and feel like they lag on forever because of it. I would take another class with her again, but I wouldn't necessarily seek her out

She is one of the best prof at SFU. Her practice exams are similar to the midterm and the final. She is very caring and understanding. Records her lectures too. I don't understand the bad ratings - if you can't get a good grade with her then maybe consider different major.

The Best Professor in SFU like actually 100%. Her midterms and final exam were based on material from lectures and problem sets assignments. She is so caring for students and very nice person. Her teaching is clear and understandable! Take with her only MBB cours!

First of all, MBB 222 in itself is a very heavy course in terms of learning so much in so little time. Dr. Li definitely eases off the load by giving clear lectures and provide tons of practice material for exams. She cares about her students and wants everyone to do well! For someone who rarely went to lectures I did very well on the midterms.

During my time at SFU, few prof's have stood out like Dr. Li. She's caring, clear, and genuinely wants her students to succeed. Her lectures are straightforward and her exams are fair. If you put in the work, you'll do well. Honestly, the negative reviews seem to come from people who didn't try because she gives you everything you need to succeed.

Dr. Li is genuinely the best professor I've had at SFU. Her slides are clear and very informative. She is so passionate about teaching and is very approachable. She tries her very best to make learning fun. MBB is a tough course since there's so much to memorize, but I'm so glad I took this class with her. Do the problem sets and you'll do good!

The best Professor I have had at sfu. Her lecture slides are great and she explains everything carefully. She provides practice exams and problem sets along with answers keys, all of which are great sources of studying and set you up for success.

prof yueh li is really kind and caring. she genuinely takes into consideration the feedback from students. someone mentioned changing midterm dates but she only did that to be considerate for us!!! practice sets for 'completion' and practice midterms like come on it doesnt get as good as this. She is really helping us, thx Dr. li.

Professor Li is the best! She is so kind, helpful and really wants her students to do well. We are so lucky to have her. Literally the fact that we have access to practice midterms that are pretty much the same as the actual midterm is unbelievable. Just show up, try, do the practice stuff and you are going to do just fine. Thanks professor.
She is one of the best profs I've ever had at sfu. She is super nice and her lectures are amazing. Her exams are really fair, as long as you go to lectures and do the practice exams.

Dr. yueh has been so helpful in this class. I was worried about taking this with another prof. glad we have her teaching us. also, I think the other ratings here with some 'tough' words should probably give their head a shake. accountability starts with ourselves before anybody. Midterm #2 was a fair test and she really guided us well towards it.

Professor Yueh told us up front the second midterm would be harder because of the topics. she didn't try to hide anything. all the practice material and her helpfulness makes this course so much more manageable. Just wrote the second midterm today and I felt really prepared based on her lecture notes detail, support and the TAs in this course.

Dr. Li is very sweet and accommodating (intended to move midterm to the end of the week to give us more time, kept the original date due to feedback, which i think is the source of some of the saltiness here). Slides are dense + frequently updated but her communication/organization of the material + exams is better than other recent teachers of 222
Her slides are riddled with mistakes and incorrect information

read the 222 reviews and tbh Alison is so nice and helpful. dunno what people are saying, probably r not mbb students so wanna get things handed to them. we know that Alison is a god-send for us her expectations and testing is more than fair. I hear other profs who teach this course do not even have recordings or practice midterms. take the win.

Dr. Li is awesome.super passionate, caring, genuinely cares about the students and success. Always trying to keep the students in mind and provide resources to succeed. lucky to have her for 222, the other rates don't know what they are talking about, maybe if they looked at themselves in the mirror it would be better. Would take again for sure.

Regarding Alison Li. Very rushed lectures and course content. She often has to correct herself the next lecture after you have already learned the wrong material. She also is very incompassionate towards people's circumstances. She has even moved midterm dates around multiple times leading to further frustrations. Overall would not recommend.

Her midterm was easy but now she can't even teach us the content properly. Her slides are a mess and she's always teaching with a different version of the ones uploaded on canvas. Too much content that she rushes through and just overall makes this course confusing for no reason.

The lectures are fundamentally horribly laid out and consists of walls of nonsense in an order that does not flow well. Concepts are covered with the grace and elegance of a high school Spanish skit translated back into English and she covers the material by reading straight off the slides with much the same energy.

Spring 2023: Would not seek him out as a prof but he was ok. Slides had a ridiculous amount of information on them and He does not say a single word on the slides during the lecture. exams can have very specific questions as well. His exams were decent but there was a time crunch and tutorials were optional. He ain't that bad just very monotone.

Dr. Paetzel did a pretty good job. The exams were fairly easy, but there was a ton of material to memorize. The lectures were pretty dry at times, but the content was actually interesting. Dr. Paetzel was always available after class to answer any questions students might have.
As I prepare for this second midterm I am realizing this degree ain't for me.

Professor Paetzel is a good prof and fairly accessible since he is always available for questions after the lecture. You can tell that he is very passionate about proteins but the lectures are sometimes dry. Make sure to understand and memorize the slides and you will be set! Also tutorials really help you understand the material.

Dr. Paetzel was never accessible outside of lecs and the TAs weren't too helpful in answering questions. Not enough practice and very heavy lectures, so most of the course had to be self-taught. Exam questions were asked vaguely but required an answer identical to the answer key. Almost an impossible A regardless of how much content you know.

Dr. Paetzel wasn't bad but also not great by any means. His lectures are very monotone and definitely not for everyone, though I didnt mind them. His test questions come directly off his slides - and are either 1) incredibly easy or 2) wildly difficult and specific. Overall, I'd take a course with him again but I wouldn't seek him out as a prof.

Lecture slides are more helpful than his actual live lectures, assignments are literally just regurgitating lecture slides, exams only cover a fraction of the material so you'll waste time studying things that wont even be tested and mark will not respond to any questions. The class isn't too hard but you'll be teaching yourself

dr. paetzel is a very intelligent person however lectures based on anything besides his specialty of proteins can be hard to watch. He has very detailed lecture slides but will go through the whole lecture without reading any of the words on them. If you study outside of class time doing textbook readings and studying the lec notes you will be fine

As others mentioned , he is ghost. Very dry and boring lectures & slides. He didn't reply to single emails. Assignments are all summarizing lectures. You have to watch his lectures and write summarization. and they grade assignment based on grammer like Engl class. No practice Qs , No preparation for midterms. Also TAs are useless. AVOID at any cos

Don't expect Dr. Paetzel to respond to your emails. He is a ghost outside of class time. He has very good slides, but the lectures are useless cause he just reads them. The exams are also not marked fairly; if you don't write the answer key exactly, you'll be marked off. Avoid him if you can!

Tutorials have no structure and the course overall is super exam heavy. It would be expected that the prof would provide some form of preparation such as extra assignments. Overall, the lectures are very thorough and interesting.

Avoid this course with Dr. Paetzel. He is not accessible, does not reply to emails, lectures are very disorganized and dry. Most assignments are very short and ask for Summarizing his lectures rather than providing practice for exams. Moreover, assignments are due the day of the exam. Would recommend waiting for another professor.

A lot of people don't really understand Dr. Paetzel, but he really cares about his students and their well-being. He wants to make sure that everyone understands what is being taught, and does a very good job of explaining the material. There are weekly assignments for completion marks, and exams are on par with assignments in terms of difficulty.
Rarely answered questions students had, and basically required you to memorize his information-heavy lectures slides in order to get a decent grade (not a great way to learn the material).

Mark is one of the nicest profs!! He was extremely understanding and takes mental health very seriously. His exams are all based off his lecture slides, so I recommend memorizing all the slides and understanding those concepts. I barely used the textbook at all. Also, I would say that the difficulty of the exams is moderate.

Mark is the sweetest professor ever! He doesn't reply to emails, but he has open discussion boards where you can ask questions, tries to answer questions during class time and hosts office hours once a week. Test material is fair and forces you to apply concepts to demonstrate your understanding. *I took this course remotely due to the pandemic*

I don't know why some reviews say that he is approachable. Well, let me tell you he's not. Doesn't answer emails about course-related content. Normally has office hours right after class, which is no good because students have other classes too. Canvas quizzes are worded poorly, good for reading practice, the meaning of the quiz is unmined.

He is a terrible prof. Not only that, he isn't very understanding of his students, kind of indifferent to them like they are part of an experiment he doesn't really care about? I honestly think profs like him shouldn't be allowed to teach, as having basic social skills should be a requirement to be a professor.

He is seriously the WORST PROF EVER! He can't even keep the class' attention for more than 1 minute. After the first minute, half the class is already gone, and the other half has fallen asleep. He is completely out of touch with his students and it's clear he doesn't get out of his lab much, and maybe he should just stay there to save all of us?

Although his lectures may seem boring but he has a depth knowledge of what he teaches. His exams are straight forward from the lecture notes. Make sure you study the diagrams in the notes in and out. He likes proteins so much. Tutorial are very helpful. He got a great team of TAs. Overall, he is a good prof.

Can't blame the guy for having boring lectures. I mean there really is no other way to talk in an interesting way about the stuff we go through. Hes a nice guy and puts in a bit of dry humor. Exams were rather hard and went into great detail. Textbook was really helpful in this class.

His first time teaching 222, didnt know how to make the first exam, but got better after. His lec are extremely boring, have to read the slide before talking most of the time. Be sure to study the notes well and the previous mts. His questions are straight of the notes, and he will a few questions that go into more depth. He loves his bonds.

His exam questions are usually from the lecture notes and the tutorial questions. That being said, this is not an easy class because there is A LOT of memorization! However, his notes are really good so you don't need the textbook. He is always open to helping people with any questions. Nice guy, he's sort of like a cute grandpa.

His ability to lecture is not so great (boring) however he is a very nice professor - seems to be willing to help students succeed. His tests are based on his PowerPoint slides, as well as tutorial assignments, so really pay attention to those! Midterm and final was fair, not too hard, but not too easy. Having a good memory would definitely help.

Overly detailed lecture slides and ridiculously dry lectures. If you are an auditory learner avoid taking the class with him. That being said his tests were very fair, and he posted great review packages. One of those classes where you need to learn everything outside of lecture.
Really nice prof! He really cares about his students and puts a lot thought into his lectures. His exams are also straightforward and easy to do well on if you study his lecture notes.

Great course, great professor, although his lectures are a bit on the dry side. If you fall asleep easy, you may want to stick to another professor. But on the bright side, he records his lectures so that helps A LOT! Warning: TA's will be your biggest obstacle. Tutorial questions very difficult. Good practice for exams though. Like!

Great prof! Lectures are fairly dry, but he is VERY informative. All lecture recordings are online, which I found to be EXTREMELY helpful. He is very willing answer questions and clarify difficult topics for you. Midterm/final are straight from the notes! His review slides cover the main focuses on his exams. Dont worry if u find tutorial sets hard
Really nice prof.Study off lecture notes, and make sure not to get lazy by the end of the course. Final exam is a must study for.

Do not buy the textbook for the course if you have him! All you need to study for his midterms/final is his lecture notes and the practice problems. Read/complete those and you'll do fine in this course! He's a super nice professor, and easily approachable. The lectures are super boring, but overall worth going to. Tutorials however were useless.

This prof is great if you like to read just the slides and don't like to read the text. However, he spends just a little more time looking at his notes than the students and interacting with them. Also, he goes thru examples so make sure you pay great attention then!!but in general very nice and approachable!
His notes/slides are good. Just memorize his notes and you'll do very well in this class! :) He's a little monotone but he's very knowledgeable and approachable. His exams are VERY fair!

Professor was really fair, can't blame him for my performance. Lecture notes were great, maybe not the most interesting prof but without attending lecture, if you strictly study his notes, you could ace this course. Wish i had a second shot at it

People were saying that he is hard for Mbb222, after I taking it!he is boring in class, and he look Scientist ?? but, he grades marks so fair and exam is pretty much from lecture note, and he told you what is coving in exam and what is not. so is

This guy is the best prof - very fair and concise.  He is intelligent, knowledgeable, considerate, etc. and actually cares about the course. Anyone who thinks otherwise is either a complete dumb ass or doesn't know what they're talking about.

BORING! This guy cannot explain ANYTHING clearly. His notes are hard to read and lack logic and clarity.  When you come to his office hours with a simple question, you will never get a straight answer; he will stare at you and ask you questions back.
lectures are pretty borning but if you memorize the notes you will do great in the class. Get over 90% on final and automatic A+ (always nice).Pretty clear on information, just monotone.
So boring! long exams!
Not bad, material in the course is pretty straightforward so not too hard to do well.  His quiz/midterms are fair (although a bit long) but final was hard!  Lectures = naptime.
crazy hairstyle, fair grader

Dr. Sinclair's notes are very straightforward... they just have to be memorized and you can do great in the course. However, the lectures are very boring and easy to fall asleep in since he is very monotone.
Makes me fall aspleep almost every lecture. :(
No Comments

MBB 222 in fall 2025.
Cons:
-immense amount of content
-Difficult to know which topics to focus on
Pros:
-Good lecturer 
-Lectures were provided online with visuals and were an excellent resource
-Piazza was an amazing resource and think all classes should have it, Dr. Young and the the TA was very helpful in clarifying concepts and expectations

if you are taking this class to move forward with your major. Do NOT take this class with this man. Wait for another semester. You will be completely overwhelmed. You wouldn't even know how to study for his class. He gives way too many materials, labelling as examinable. His tests are the worst. I repeat, avoid this man.

slides and objective lists very messy. didn't bother reading the textbook bc i couldn't find the pages on my edition. exams are difficult, and i didn't understand where the hw questions came from. even if you memorize everything you probably won't answer the questions right.

Dr Young's lectures are jumbled and disorganized. He is passionate about his work but it translates terribly into lecture slides with lacking explanations. Tests are marked harshly based on certain terminology words. Save your time in lecture and read the textbook and slides by yourself.

Out of all the profs I've had at sfu he is the worst one. lectures were not helpful for me. Exams were extremely badly worded, many people I know lost points for the wording of the questions. Felt like I wasn't graded for what I actually knew unfortunately.

Just don't bother. As a 2nd year student nothing can prepare you to write 45 minute exams out of 80 marks. The lectures are extremely difficult to understand and he does not propose any hints as to what may appear on the exams. The level of difficulty on some of the exams is through the roof, and even harder grading done by the TA's.

his lectures were like a heart attack because he would do practice problems and then ask ppl in class to answer questions, super hard to follow him in lecture so i ended up self teaching the entire course. he provides objective lists which were super useful for that but exams were def hard. lol do not take this w him if u wanna get a good grade

I liked his explanation tbh, he made lectures interesting. But boyy the exams!! The TA's didn't even know what his qestions were asking! His exams were so difficult for no reason. He puts fill in the blank? like dude wth how on earth am I supposed to know what you want me to write. Both midterms were graded wrongly, had to argue to get points.

Lectures are difficult to sit through, and his lecture slides are incredibly crowded and unorganized. His tests are exactly like his quizzes, but it's unclear how to study efficiently for the types of questions he asks. Would not recommend.
horrible prof. needs to get his lectures in check. just cause you understand them doesnt mean anyone else will, also going to lecture is useless and overall worst prof at sfu

going to lecture is a major waste of time but the slides are confusing on their own so pick your poison. he doesn't upload the notes ahead of time which would've been fine if he wasn't so hard to follow. half the time you can't hear him and the other half he rambles on and on about things you don't even need to know. just take it with a diff prof

His office hours were amazing (probs why I got an A+). He asks you questions so you can actually understand the material. His lectures are pointless to go to. Read the lectures, go to office hours, and do objective list (practice test not that similar). His tests require you to very much know the material via lectures and the objective list.

Lectures and slide decks are unclear and confusing--he goes off topic a lot during the lecture. Make sure to keep up with the objectives from day 1; they are like a vocabulary list to understand the heavy science jargon in his slides. His class is quite messy and unorganized. Avoid him if possible!!

Dr. Young is a good prof and cares that his students do well. His slides are messy but they contain all the information needed. He is very clear about the testable material and provides lots of practice. At the beginning of every lecture he did open review on the previous lectures content. I recommend taking MBB 222 with him.
Wanted very specific answers for the exam, honestly didn't learn much in lecture. And you will definitely be picked on in lecture.

I really like Dr. Young. I understand some of the ratings because some of the test questions can be confusing(material is hard). BUT he is a great prof, he will sit at office hours with you and really explain things, he also wants you to understand and be capable of explaining things back to him. He's not a “memorize” type of prof.

I honestly really enjoy his class. He provides clear objectives for each week, and only the items on that list are examinable. His exams are fair, he records his lectures, and he is very accessible. The only downside is that the exams can feel a bit rushed, but overall, he is a very good professor. He is passionate and explains things well.

I really liked Dr. Young, he was always there to help outside of class, his lecture material is bit unorganized but if you actually sort through it all of the information is there and its very thorough. His exams are very fair and if you actually go through all the lectures it's all there for you! I would definitely recommend taking it with him!!
takes advantage of students, gets away with it bc he's also a chair associate

RUDE. no care bt you as a student no matter the situation. MESSY slides, inconsistent in marking, only likes his way of teaching, will give you some notes but says they won't be relevant/what you're going to be tested on. 
his response to ANYTHING asked or any type of help/accommodation "see course policies" "contact TA"
Self-teach and stay away

Whenever given the chance, take a class with Dr.Young, it'll really change the way you think and study biology. Dr.Young makes you find the logic and reasoning behind something to understand it rather than simply memorize. This is the most (and best) I've learnt anything bio related, and it has stuck with me. This class despite the stress was fun.

Taking MBB22 with Dr. Young was really interesting and one thing for sure, it changed the way I will ever study biology. He puts more emphasis on insights and the logical reason behind any event, instead of memorizing everything by rote. Another great thing was the writing assignments, it definitely gave me an idea on how to write scientifically.

Dr. Young is very knowledgeable, truly cares for students, and makes an effort to prepare materials for class. But if you decide to take a class with him be prepared to read from the first session. The main problem is that his course note is not suitable for his exams. However, he also posts really helpful archive notes.

Dr.Young isn't a bad prof. ONLY bad thing was he gave 45 min in midterms and for each part on the final (2 parts) instead of 50 min (and 3 hours for final) - there was barely enough time to finish even if you knew every question. If you do the readings, assignments, and go to class, you can do well BUT you have to be fast on exams
He should not be a prof. Have never come across someone as bad as him
His lectures left me more confused about the content and his slides were all over the place. It was easier learning from the slides created by other professors for this course.

the presentations were all over the place. he made the lectures difficult. his class was not enjoyable with all the stress added from 'do it yourself' situation. his tests seemed bit unfair with less time and more question, if you didn't read anything properly, you lost marks. Don't take this class if you want to actually learn.

This class is more work than an organic chemistry class and lab combined.

Every week theres an objective list that requires you to read a sht ton of textbook pages and all sorts of slides and website links to answers the questions on the objective lists and extra exercises given.

Exams were fair if you managed to survive the work.
one of the most horrific classes I've taken

He complicates things when his whole job is supposed to be breaking it down for his students. It's like he wants us to be more confused or something?? I should have listened to the reviews on here, never taking a professor with a low rating again I'm genuinely traumatized.
He is very nice but man you dont have time to think during any of his midterms. Its either you know the answer the first time you read it or you dont.
Just don't
Avoid at all costs.

The professor is nice, but his lectures are not helpful. You have to do a lot of extra reading outside of class time in order to understand what is covered during the lectures. The lecture slides are not helpful either, and consist of way too many things on a single slide in a random order. Avoid taking this class with this professor.

Prof. Young is simultaneously the nicest guy ever and the worst professor. His pdfs and lectures are confusing, and the TA is zero help. Young is so enthusiastic about the material and so likeable (which normally makes the class bearable) but seriously, he is literally the worst professor I have ever had. Take it with another professor if you can.

Excellent professor, provides you with everything you need to succeed: practice, excellent lecture slides, involved iClickers. He even took off some marks from the total of all 3 of the exams. But most importantly he is very knowledgable and very good at explaining things. Wonderful prof, if I could, I would take all of my MBB courses with him.

The lecture notes are very sparse and disorganized, with him often jumping between 4 documents on the overhead. Its better to try to learn lecture material outside of class, but attendance is mandatory due to IClicker. When asked questions during office hours he would just tell us to post the questions on piazza instead of helping in person. 0/5
Great professor. Makes you do the work but the exams are exactly what he requires you to read and learn. No tricks involved. 10/10 would take again.

Dr Young gives you all that you need, straight up. Once you get what you have to do, which is a decent load for a course, then you're set. Follow his instructions on how to study, come to lectures for iClickers, do your readings(provided page by page) and you'll have a breeze with his course! Not your regular approach to teaching but effective.

He's a bit odd. Didn't like him at first, but as the semester progressed, he kinda grew on me. iclickers for participation every class. Slides organized weirdly with the iclickers right smack in the middle of the lecture. Uploads pdf files TO NO END. Txtbook is your friend. The entire course seems randomly organized.

Edgar uses iClickers so it forces you to go to class, but sadly, his lectures are purely useless and he goes off-topic occasionally.  His attitude towards students was quite poor and condescending as well. If I had to learn all the material myself, I wish the course would've been offered online instead.

I'm paraphrasing here, but Dr. Young stated early on that "he's not here to teach us the material, he's here to tell you how to teach yourself".  See, he originally taught MBB 222 online but decided to reuse his learning guides for the in person class so get ready to learn 100% from the textbook. Didn't learn anything in lecture. Had a bad time.

Have to learn everything from the textbook.  Lectures are a waste of time.  He makes "learning objectives" but goes over 10% of material required for the course.  I understand mbb is very difficult, and there is a lot of material, but he wastes time in class being condescending.  I'm a good student, and would recommend avoiding if possible.

In my experience, you really have to work for your grade. This class is basically a self learning course. If you don't like how Dr. Young runs his class, he literally tells you in the beginning of the semester that it's going to be like that every class. Overall, I feel that he is a fair prof as he takes off 15% of your worst quizzes and iclicker.

MBB 222 is itself a very heavy load course meaning the lectures aren't enough time for the prof to go over everything. However Dr. Young's learning guides are all you need, memorize all the vocab and your set. He also offers bonus marks on his exams which help a ton! Would definitely recommend!

Dr. Young did not TEACH us anything! Rather than going through slides about general, important topics, he would focus on minute details of the course content, leaving those who hadn't read ahead lost. Homeworks and iclickers were given on content we had not touched on in class. Midterms were fair, final BRUTAL.

Lecture was completely useless. Spent 80% of class answering student Q's & 1 or 2 Iclicker questions. Be prepared to self-teach yourself ALL the material. Had AMAZING TAs, tutorials super helpful. MT1&2 were reasonable (had 90%+ on MT's) but Final was ridiculous, left 1/3 blank. Prof is condescending & rude to students. Avoid at all costs.
Lectures are useless but you have to come because of iCliker participation marks. TAs and online resources really help.

DO NOT take this class with Professor Young! Minimal material is covered in class, so you have to spend countless hours at home teaching yourself everything that he didn't cover in lecture for every unit. Be ready to do LOTS of reading if you are taking this class with him as there are no lecture slides.

He barely teaches in class so you are expected to learn everything yourself from the textbook. The only reason I ever went to class was because of his iclickers that counted for marks. STAY AWAY from him at ALL COSTS.   He couldn't answer any questions properly and even his TA's were constantly confused as to what he wanted us to know for exams.
he made me lost all the interests about MBB, i was even wondering if i should change my major. since MBB222 is the very first MBB courses to start
Very very bad prof. Try to avoid at all costs
he's the worst prof... never teach anything, just enjoying his own jokes in class and i learned nothing from him, all i have to is to read the books and self-study, useless to go to the lecture.
Very knowledgeable and helpful during office hours. Be prepared to work for your grade.
great prof :D he has a high interest in DNA & RNA..especially DNA!! so i guess he's suited to teach this mbb class since 80% of this class is elaborated on DNA and RNA

he is an awesome prof. don't miss out on the chance to have him as a prof! yeah he talked pretty fast, but if you actually pay attention to what he says, the stuff is easy. a lot of ppl said the final was hard, it sort of was :P but i really enjoyed his class!

super awesome notes, but talks a bit too fast during lecture. I found going to lecture made info stick much easier than having to learn on ur own. approachable. SUPER FAIR MARKING and really, there are no surprises. I don't know what people are complaining about. minimal effort got me a B. If u did poorly, u prolly missed the big picture...

Terrible exam writer-- midterm was ridiculously easy and general, then the final was the other extreme-- obscure details drawn from perhaps 1% of the course content. Controlling prof with little interest in helping students learn and succeed.
No Comments
Great lectures, easy to understand. Exams are fair
Hands down the best prof I have ever got in my 2 years undergrad experience. Dustin is my kingg!

I highly recommend taking a course w/ dr.king!! Great guy, not a bad thing to say about him. I def recommend that if u can tach ochem1 first, taking them at the same time can be really hard since everything will require at adequate understanding of ochem and many fall behind bc of it

Dustin is the goat. Great guy once you get to know him and always willing to help in office hours. MBB 222 is a dense course but he does well to concise it and try and make the course interesting for students. pay attention to his lectures and slides and you should be good to go. I would take any course with him for sure
Honestly goated prof. Very clear what you needed to know and exams were super fair. Assignments were not too difficult either.
Amazing prof, def would love to take again if possible, hope he teaches more upper level courses in the future.

10/10 prof. Lots of content and lec heavy, but does an amazing job at simplyifying all of it. Final was harder than the midterms, but expected due to a lot more in depth content, still fair though if you studied. Never did anything extra (office hours, tutorting, tutorials,etc.) only listened to lecs and did amazing in the class

There are a lot of notes to take from lecture, so I recommend taking notes on top of the lecture slides if possible. The textbook isn't totally necessary, but helpful if you want to perform really well. The exams are pretty reasonable, but also very very rushed, so watch out for that!

Dr. King is one of the kindest, most knowledgeable, and accessible profs there are at SFU. The bad reviews he gets are because MBB 222 is a really dense course no matter how you go about it. Get your CHEM 281 first to help you survive. I had to put in work for this course and went to my TA's office hours every week but in the end, it was worth it.
He is a very considerate professor and he explains things very clearly. The class itself isn't easy but he's a fair marker so if you study enough you'll do fine.

You need to go to class and actually pay attention because he tends to focus on specific topics that you can't get from the lecture slides alone and those things WILL pop up on the exam. The only thing about his testing is you get 40 MINS for both midterms, and that is NOT ENOUGH but other than that his tests are pretty fair. Don't fall behind

this is the first time im writing a ratemyprof review and its just because dr king was literally the best!! i was initially nervous about taking the class because of the reviews but honestly if you study his slides (know them well) this class won't be too bad. it is content heavy, yes, but dr. king made it really interesting and I loved it!

I took this course at the same time as chem281 and found the first exam quite difficult, as the material requires a decent amount of understanding of Ochem. However, Dr. King explains concepts well and I was able to get my grade up later on. Lots of memorization for a lot of material, and a huge time crunch for midterms.

you can tell this guy enjoy what we studies and teaches. Very good at explaining difficult concepts and even records lectures to look back on just in case. His practices midterms can appear “easier” than the actual midterms, but the only thing i didnt enjoy were the time crunches made for the exams. Other than that, great dude!

Dr. King was an amazing professor. He's very passionate about what he teaches, which allows him to teach the material really well. He tests based on what he teaches and emphasizes, and is really clear on what you need to know

Dr King is a nice guy who seems to be very knowledgeable about the topics, as he answered every question asked in class. The two midterms are very time-crunchy with there only being 40 minutes. The final is the same format, just with 3 hours so it gives you time to review your work. MBB 222 has too much content, but I think Dr King did a good job.

I'm taking chem 281 with this course so I feel like it may distort my perception on him. Honestly, the tests are decently fair but there is so much content and a little more than half an hour to finish the midterms (which i hate) I often have to fight for my grade to get above average so I wouldn't recommend mbb 222 as a co req w/ chem 281

Explains so clearly, great lecturer, very charismatic. Exams are fair enough, though there are sometimes small bits out of nowhere that were not focused on in lecture. Overall great prof, relatively fair exams.

Dr kings classes have a good atmosphere and his lectures can be engaging if you're interested in the subject. the midterms are fair,doesn't have enough time but you can get a good grade if you go over the lecture materials thoroughly. Final was harder than the midterms, more challenging questions. Overall would recommend taking this course with him

He is a great professor, he seems very passionate about what he teaches. The course itself is very heavy on material so you are going to have to memorize a lot of things. His tests are not too hard but you do need to study a lot for them. Overall really good professor.
Excellent lectures and very approachable. If you work hard and commit to learning the material you will do very well in this course. Assessments are very fair and straight forward.

overall a nice person. doesnt know how to teach "everything is testable" practice mts r nothing like the actual midterms just memorize everything in his slides dont even try to understnad cuz you wont.

Prof King is a great lecturer and explains all material very well. Course is super loaded with material and practice midterms are not a reflection of what exam will be like. Exams are pretty tough and require a lot of attention to each MC question.

Did so bad on the 2 midterms but I locked in for the final. All you need is hard work and dedicate your soul to studying. Lecture heavy, too many things to memorize. Overall I think he is good at teaching and would recommend him if you actually put in the work.

Difficult and not good at teaching. Does Not respond to emails regardless of email subject. Tests dont give students enough time and questions are unnecessarily confusing. Overall would avoid if possible.

nice guy but horrible prof. he expects us to memorize so much bc "everything is testable". his practice mts were way easier and nothing like the actual mts so don't think ur ready when ur actually not esp bc his mts are only 40 minutes long with 40 marks.

He is a nice person. However, slides are loaded with so much information and he will never say what to study or focus more on for exams. He will just say everything is testable and then memorizing all that he puts on his slides is hell.

a lot of details to remember, anything in the slides or that he talked about can be on the exam, don't fall behind, and mock exams are usually a lot easier than the real exams. very passionate and likeable guy though

Decent professor that provides lecture recordings and seems passionate about his stuff. Course is densely packed with details so try to remember most of it bc "everything can be tested". 40 minute midterms were very rushed, but the final was not too bad.

MBB is a heavy content course, and while King tried his best to teach it, its hard to stay focused in his class for the whole semester. His exams are 40 mins long which is kinda short for the amount of questions he gives us. 6/10 prof.

Passionate, enthusiastic, and seems to enjoy teaching the course. The course is very material-heavy: lots of information to learn and understand. Midterms are only 40 minutes long which in my opinion, is too short. The extra 10 minutes can help obtain better marks. Slides are very dense, many of them containing unnecessary information.

Lectures are very dense so it can get tough to stay focused the whole time but Dr King does a good job going through the material thoroughly. Very receptive and encouraging to questions during lecture. Tests are very detail oriented and will get very specific.

Lectures may be dry but if you pay attention, he explains things really well. Listen to lecture recordings if you run out of time to study! Read the textbk if you can. Exams were generally not too difficult for me. MT2 was a lil tricky but I expected it to be so as it was all multiple choice (due to strike). I can tell he cares for his students!

professor King is amazing considering that it was his first semester teaching I think he was wonderful. I must say his first midterm was light work and then he DUCKED US UP with the second midterm it was very difficult but then his third was ok. ALL IM SAYING IS ACTUALLY understand everything on his slides thats ur way to an A. His final is fair.

This was Dr. King's first semester as a prof and I really enjoyed his class. You can tell he loves his field. 
I recommend listening back to the lecture recordings and using the textbook as an extra resource. I wouldn't say he's a tough marker but he wants understanding. He tests content taught the class before the midterm but overall he's fair.

Nice guy overall and his first year teaching. Seems really in the topics. His midterm averages were listed in another rating. His second midterm was definitely unfair and should have been graded less as it was during TA strikes and it was all MC. Otherwise the average would have been about 65%. Definitely try to keep up but overall is very fair.
He's nice, hard midterms especially the multiple choice. The averages for the 3 midterms were 73.6%, 57.8% and  67.5%.
Dustin was a great prof, fair midterms but course is VERY content heavy. Be prepared to read and study.
Our king :) good bless this man he is so slay :) 100% recommend but get rdy to read cuz his slides are full of pictures and diagrams

Dr.King is one of the sweetest most caring professors I have ever had. His first and third midterm were very fair and his second was hell on earth ( I was mad) but generally speaking he is rdy to answer all ur Qs and he is super nice so reach out if u need help. The sweetest man alive. If u wanna succeed then read ur  textbook

All the midterms were quite hard and had lots of info that could be from textbook or lecture. This course is very content heavy so be prepared to study a lot. Practice midterms provided were much easier than the midterms so dont be fooled.

The first midterm was just O-chem review, the second midterm was just hard as it was all challenging mc and the third final was a bit better than the second one. This is not an easy course, keep up and use your TAs for help. Practice midterms make you think you're ready when you're not actually ready for the midterm's level of difficulty.
Lots of reading because material can be pulled from textbook for midterms. Dustin is a great prof and answers questions clearly but can be boring at times in lecture.

A researcher maybe, an instructor? No. Was not able to convey ideas clearly and focused too heavily on memorization especially considering the course was supposed to be concept based. I ended up getting an A- without ever taking a single note in his class, relying purely on self-study sessions.

Prof is really passionate about his field, and I believe works hard to ensure the success of his students. He tries to make this class seem interesting, as material is quite dry. Notes are detailed but not overly so. That being said, it's unfortunate that his thick french accent makes it very hard to understand him. Avoid if you can't self study.

If you have trouble paying attention in class or making your own notes I advise to stay away from this prof. The TA in this class is what saved my grade and he most likely wrote the exams which were ok. I am not sure if this prof will teach again as there were other profs in his lectures marking what he was doing which is never good. Thick accent!
Decent prof. Hard to understand but it doesnt matter just study on your own and youll be fine. exams are straight from lecture notes, problem sets and tutorial questions.

Avoid this prof if you can. Lectures were extremely boring due to his monotone voice and French accent. Lecture slides get posted before lecture and are complete, but they are basically copy and pasted from the textbook and lengthy.. definitely not 'notes'. Seems like he's passionate about the material, but not interested in teaching.

Dr. Pio is a decent professor, he knows his stuff, and he's very intellectual with his teaching. He's not the one who makes up the exams, it's his TA's. With that said, the course material is very dense and dry, and you need to study in order to understand. The TA's do a good job of conferring exam-related info. Other than that, course is okay.

AT ALL COSTS, DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS WITH HIM. He may be the worst prof I have ever encountered, in terms of teaching ability and understanding. His french accent is such a determent, which can be incredibly hard for many people to understand.  His ability to imply the concept of each lecture is terribly weak.

Save yourself the time and DO NOT TAKE THE CLASS WITH HIM! Terrible prof, cannot understand him at all in class and makes material very confusing. His notes are impossible to understand which makes it even worse... The guy seems like he just wants to do research and doesn't care about teaching
Aweosome notes! Very funny guy takes off the tension of being in a MBB class. Final was long! So work fast. He's one of the few MBB prof's who has a good sense of humour. Good Luck!
Very good prof. Very fair. The final was a little too long but not overally difficult. Overall, good prof.
Good professer, very helpful to the students, note have a lot of extra info that is sometimes hard to decifer through
Dr. Craig is one of the best. She has very clear explanation of concepts. Her lecture slides are very informative, so you won't need to do much textbook reading. Overall, great prof!

Lisa Craig is a nice prof who'll answer your inquiries via email quickly. Her slides'll have everything you need to know, so you can skimp out on class. That being said-- they have quite a bit of extra information as well, attending would help you sift through what you really need to know. The class is undoubtedly a lot of work but very passable.

Very fair. You don't need the textbook, exams are 100% based on lecture powerpoints. Lecture were really dry, and didn't usually add anything that wasn't in the uploaded slides. Homework heavy (weekly assignments) but it was actually helpful in learning the material. Not hard to get a good grade if you're willing to put in the time

You can tell she's passionate about the course but lectures can be dry and there's a lot of information to absorb but she tells you everything you need to know on her lecture slides. No textbook needed. Midterms were very fair no surprises. Really enjoyed the course with her!

Her lecture slides are long but that's all you need to know. Book is pretty much useless for this course, 100% of the test comes from her lecture slides. Do tutorial assignments and quizzes because that's like a bonus 20 % for your grade.
Good lecture slides. Everything you need should be there. Don't need to textbook. Don't slack like I did...

Tests were very fair.  Lectures are well organized,  she doesn't rush the content,  it's a very manageable class.  Her lectures slides I would recommend making your own notes as hers are very long winded.  But they contain all the info you need! Didn't really use the textbook,  slides and problem sets were enough.

Her lecures are are hard to keep listening to because there's a lot of material and can be dry. Lecture slides are perfect for studying if you have the patetience to read all of it (there's a lot of words per slide). Tests are pretty easy and questions don't require as much detail as you would expect for mbb. she cares about her students!

Her lectures seem dry at first but you really have to find a way to listen cause her notes are amazing. The notes are super detailed but thats ALL you need to know. No surprises on exams. She makes it very clear what you need to know and what you don't. I loved her, she was super helpful and sweet.

Her notes are very detailed so there isn't much of a need for the textbook except to reaffirm concepts. Her exams are very fair; she expects you to know the big picture rather than the gritty details. Though she mainly reads off her slides during lectures, she is very thorough while going through them.
Fair marking, and great explanations

She is smart and knows what is relavent to the course, but she is one of the countless teachers who have been destroyed by powerpoint.
 
 spends far too much time just reading us her powerpoints.. would be much better if she just used it as a launch pad to her own thoughts.

Great prof until now in MBB department. MBB is a hard subject, but she gives you lots of hints for exams. Text is not useful at all, you will need it for 322. I got lots of helps during her office hours.

Her lectures are dry and boring and most of the time she doesn't even know what she's talking about. Midterms are fair but the final came out of nowhere! She will ask you about the tiniest details in the notes. I wasn't a big fan of MBB to start with and she just made it worse. So if find MBB to be very hard, don't take it with her.

Probably the worst instructor I have every had. It was evident that her powerpoints were "borrowed" from previous instructors - and that was reflected when the exams did not match the material covered in class. She was also completely unavailable for help, and told the class on multiple occasion " do not email me for anything ".

oOverall good teacher. Her notes are great and detailed so you barely have to touch the book. Tip: watch her youtube videos (she'll provide links in her notes) as a few questions on the final will hit what was highlighted in the videos. Tests definitely fair and not too detailed, just a little tricky with wording

another so-so prof for the biochem department. maybe its her first time teaching this course, and i dunno if its the prof or the material, her class just doesnt arouse much interest towards mbb in me. her lecture is so boring that pillow or coffee is a neccesity. the notes are OK (though she didnt make the notes herself).

Great professor, exams are more than fair (on the easy side but maybe because of the easy content), explains ideas clearly and emphasizes whats going to be on the exam, don't need the textbook as her notes pretty much cover what you will need to know.
Nice.
Great prof that got excited about course content
Demanding but very knowledgeable professor that I highly respect.

I wish this course wasn't so jam-packed so we could have a chance to ask questions. Michel is a fantastic prof who cares deeply about MBB and you can clearly see that in the passion of his teaching. I loved how he took out the final and including four term tests, although they were pretty brutal. The online homework and quizzes were great too!

Prof. Leroux's lectures were dense but he explained concepts clearly. I only ever needed to use his slides for study material as they are very comprehensive. You can tell he cares about the material and also cares about his students. Tests are hard and require you to demonstrate understanding/overlap of multiple concepts.

Dr. Leroux is absolutely amazing. Exams are extremely fair as it's just memorization, expect to know lots of the detail provided in the lectures; absolutely everything ties to each other. He is passionate and caring, ALWAYS responding to your concerns. The class is set up so that you don't need to read the textbook. Class was very smooth sailing.

He is a very reasonable proff..grades u based off multiple assessments so u always have room to boost your grade. his notes are really good and precise..his exams weren't unfair..the content was fairly heavy but he taught it really well.

Michel was able to explain concepts that I had had issues understanding in other courses in ways that I was able to understand immediately. Very impressive lecturer. The class was divided into 4 midterms, online quizzes, and assignments that were just for completion, making it very easy to boost your marks in various ways. 100% recommend.

This is the worst prof I've had since being at sfu. His midterms are super rushed for time and he makes no effort to give them back. He gave an assignment and quiz due over reading week (is that even allowed). After his lectures, I can't even tell you what we've learnt because he tries to cram in so much information so fast and goes overtime.

Dr. Leroux was a great lecturer. He was always very clear and showed real passion for the material. He includes FYI sections in every set of slides that (usually) apply to the material taught, and were typically interesting. The exams online were rather rough, and his lectures could tend to be long, but overall the course was good and enjoyable.

If you put the effort in, you'll do fine. His exams are straightforward; however, doing this course online with him was a disaster and I would personally never take another online course with him. He also does not care enough to accommodate students in case of personal emergencies, even with weeks of notice.

Dr. Leroux is a good professor. The course was quite heavy, but Dr. Leroux explained things pretty clearly. Tests were fair, and weekly assignments/quizzes were easy marks and helped with studying. Slides are very text-heavy and sometimes repetitive, but contain all the info you could need.

I would recommend taking this course with him. It's a content heavy course. He uses slides with a lot of texts and pictures, so even if you don't have time to watch the lectures, the slides would be enough to get the gist of the content. Go to tutorials, memorization alone won't help, you have to fully understand the concepts. Overall a good prof.

Michel is really nice and I love the extra notes that he drafts up which are super helpful and makes the material easy to understand. He can be a bit disorganized at times but he does listen to student feedback. I would definitely consider taking another class with him!

A very reasonable prof. You decide what your grade will be depending on your habits. Keep up, and you'll do great. No trick questions. There is a lot of material but that's with any MBB course. Exams are very fair. Open book quiz and completion marked assignment every week (aka free marks). He makes lectures interesting and explains well.

explains concepts super well. lots of information to learn but it's super interesting anyway. exams are super straightforward and easy if you read his slides. studying a day or 2 before class can get you an A- on the exam. weekly quizzes and assignments help with studying and it also gives you free marks (basically an extra 20%)
Wasn't a fan of his lectures, but his tests were mostly straightforward.

A fine prof but if you can find a better one then I definitely recommend avoiding Leroux. He reads from the slides (which aren't his own) and goes really slowly in a super soft and monotone voice, which put half his class to sleep. Only reason why he was an okay prof was that his exams were fair and it wasn't too difficult to get a decent mark.

Dry content, each lecture was on average 40 slides packed full of text, which makes it a very material dense course. Most work I've ever put into a course in my life with discouraging results. Do not recommend. Not curved. Spring 2017 Final average was 58%.
Dr. Leroux is a nice guy but lectures get very dry.  LOTS of material.

Michel is a teacher who is genuinely excited about the subject. A little shy and self-deprecating, he tries hard to make the material make sense, and is always willing to go over things between class. That said, it IS an information-dense subject, so a good bit of reading and work on your own is required to do well. I found study groups helpful.

Dr. Leroux is a great prof. He made a course like MBB which is filled with details very interesting and fascinating. Truly a legend when it comes down to presenting lectures in the most effective and simplistic manner. If you go over his lecture notes thoroughly, you should be able to earn a decent grade. Had great time with him this spring!

There is a lot of material covered in MBB222, but Dr. Leroux makes an incredible effort to try and present the info as clear as possible. PP tend to be long and wordy, but if you follow thru w all the slides, you will not need to dwell in the textbook.

Honestly, I can't speak for people taking Upper Division classes with Dr. Leroux, but as someone in his MBB222 class, I think he's one of the best you can get. He can be a bit dry at times, and his lectures can be quite heavy, but that's MBB. It's a dense course and Dr. Leroux is SUPER approachable and friendly. Easiest and most friendly prof ever.

Absolutely amazing prof. He would put videos on youtube to further explain topics and show examples, and was fully acessible for extra help. Super friendly, but does expect you to work (but only as much as him-which is a lot).
A great lecturer in the MBB dept.  Would recommend to take any MBB class with him.  Not easy, but makes the material clear and concise.

Dr. Chu is amazing. This class, however is pretty hard, but you just need to put in work. He tells you what's important and what to avoid which saves a lot of time, and also has review sessions which is a major key. He actually takes time to know the people he interacts with and if very kind and helpful. 10/10 professor!

He is a fun exciting prof that most people love! He is good at explaining concepts, great at giving you  extra time outside office hours. Seems genuinely interested in the happiness and success of his students. He prepares you well for exams and the exams are pretty fair. Totally recommend him as your professor! :)

I had Eugene for MBB 222 in summer 2015 and he was amazing!! he explained everything really thoroughly, and made sure to repeat concepts that (historically) students had trouble with. His practice midterms were a really good reflection of his exams. He's patient, available, and very open to constructive criticism. hands down best prof ever :)

I had Professor Chu for MBB 222 and he was awesome! MBB can be boring but he really puts in the effort to make the class interesting, and to help you get a good grade, and if youre not doing too good, to make sure you pass. I was in his class fall 2014, the first one he was teaching, and i definitely recommend him over any of the other profs.

ALWAYS available for extra help. I did badly on a midterm and he personally emailed me to check in and invite me to his office hours to go over the material. Tough material, tough exams, tough grading (he doesn't scale), but always fair, always respectful towards students, always obviously happy to be there.

Great professor. He follows all the aspects of a good teacher and if he was doing any other course, would be my most favorite professor thus far.  Mbb 222 is a hard course, no matter who teaches it. Eugene just makes it a little easier.  Power point slides seem to come from the department, but Eugene tells you what to know, and what to ignore.
Unless you have prepared or you have extremely good skill of memorization, don't enroll in MBB 222 no matter what prof you choose.
GREAT PROF, VERY CLEAR POWERPOINT SLIDES (TEXTBOOK ISN'T REQUIRED) EXAMS ARE BASED STRICTLY OFF LECTURE NOTES, AND REQUIRES A LOT OF MEMORIZATION.
Dr. Chu is really nice, and is always open to helping you out. A lot of memorization is required which potentially makes the midterms and final hard-- they are however fair though.
Great prof, The class is very difficult in itself, but Eugene makes it somewhat straightforward. Reuses a lot of practice questions in his exams!!!

Good prof who likes to teach and wants students to do well. There is a lot to cover though so he flies through 30 - 40 slides per lecture. He will make it clear though what will come up on the exams and they were fair overall. The class kinda sucks no matter who the prof is...

Incredibly approachable. Go to his office hours and he'll explain everything really well until you fully understand; He's always willing to help. There is a lot of work required but it's biochemistry so that's not shocking. Tells you exactly what you need to know, removes as much as he can. Super fair midterms and final IF you put in the work!
He is a good prof, although the course content is a bit dense, his exams are really fair!
Very nice professor! He really tries to do what he thinks is best for his students. His tests were extremely fair.  Very approachable.
He tries his best to engage students and make lectures fun. Tests were fair. Good prof.

He seems very interested in the material but he isn't very good at explaining it to the class. He isn't specific on what to study for the midterms and exams, and his practice midterms and exams do not provide a realistic picture of the actual midterm and exam received. You definitely need to study every little thing for his class.

Dr. Chu is a  new prof, and you can definitely tell. He tries to being engaging, which is nice, but often doesn't clearly communicate what material is importantwhat needs to be known for exams. Really nice guy, tries to do what's right, just inexperienced. Over study for his exams.

Dr. Chu is an awesome prof. Made Microbiology a lot less daunting. Very friendly, approachable, and goes out of his way to help students. Also very creative with review sessions to help with studying.
DO NOT take MBB with HONDA, the lectures are awefully boring and his exams are unfair!

he likes to put problems rather then simple memorizing questions on his midterm and final. thats fine if he actually gave us some problems to practice on beforehand. whenever people would ask him for questions he would say to find them ourselves! all we did in tutorial was spend time preparing a presentation that was 15 min long.

His lectures were boring and didn't explain further than his posted notes. He was constantly distracted by people leaving etc and made comments about it mid-sentence. Tests were fair, all written answers though. Poor attitude towards students. Not very helpful.
I never see a professor who underestimates university students more than anybody else.

Worst prof ever. bad attitude. never on time for lecture. missed 6 lectures in one semester. notes are in his word, only he can understand. exam seems reasonable, but he doesn't teach much, he covers everything too briefly. bad bad bad

Dr. Bains provided extra help and encouragement to my friends and I during the semester (finally, a teacher with heart).  He made complex concepts simpler to understand.  I believe he should be the one teaching courses full time rather than being a TA.  On a side note, he loves his hockey!
Thank god Onkar was TAing the course.  He is the best TA EVER!  Without him I would be so lost.

There's an extremely long list of adjectives I can use to describe Dr. Bains...AMAZING, INCREDIBLE, AWESOME, think that should about do it. But seriously, he's the best. Had him as a TA in MBB 222 and he's th eonly reason the class was bearable. Always willing to help, crystal clear, super easy to talk to. Just an overall super nice guy!!!

All of the answers to his tests come from his notes! He is approachable and the grading scheme is very fair. Anyone who did bad in his class has them-self to blame for it. His notes are clear and he explains topics well.I would take his class again because most of the students are whining and getting worse grades giving you a better opportunity
Good lecturer, asks mostly fair questions with a couple tricky ones. Exam/midterms are short so if you make very little mistakes, you'll still end up losing a big chunk of marks.

He is a good person, but not a good prof. He literally spent the last two weeks rushing the material. We literally did 3 lectures in 50min time -- he sped through it SO fast. He exams are hard and picks on tiny details....

He is trying his best, but still, the exams aren't really fair. He tend to pick the tiny parts of his notes to put on exams. Also he taught 50% of material in first 9/10 semester and taught another 50% on the last few days. usually notes are 15-20 pages while the Last notes he spend 2 days on was 47 pages.takes forever to study,fail if ur not smart

midterms are straight from his notes. goes slowly through the material so everyone understands but speeds up a month before the final and does 3 lectures in one class. lectures are usually extremely dull and seem like they last forever. nice guy though and always willing to help. also witty.
Exams are focused on details, not the big picture. Lots of memorization.

Completely useless. I went to talk to Briscoe after class and he completely brushed me off, didn't even answer my question. The test was ridiculous, not based on notes, and he wasted a lot of lecture time. Worst professor I've ever had at SFU.

Excellent Prof. Dont buy the txt, he only tests on notes. He teaches by analogy, giving examples of well known events and how they relate to MBB. He gives ample time for finals and midterms, they border on too short. A very fair marker, if you meet with him he can perfectly explain why you lost marks. Downside: He marks spelling, so know ur stuff.

The final was RIDICULOUS. The prof tested on minute details from the course (there was an 8 mark question on introns, which we spent about 5 minutes covering in class). I went into the test with a 90% in the course, I'll be amazed if I get anything above 60% on the final.

Not using a textbook makes it way easier to study for exams...just read the notes! His lectures were always interesting . His pre-exam hotline and some of his jokes are a little awkward but, putting all that aside, he's actually really good. Easy tutorials, decently easy problem sets, and fair midterms. Study the notes! BIG TIME

Since there is absolutely no use for the very expensive textbook, he shouldn't even put it as a required text on the course outline. Students are as broke as it can be, so why make us spend 100s of dollars on an absolute piece of useless thing, when the notes are sufficient. I don't get it
Avoid him if u can.Exams are not fair.Easy MT, Very hard final, no scale.TUT and problem sets are not relevant to exams. Make sure fully understand his notes.
Does not use textbook but won't tell you at the beginning of the course. What a waste of money. No scaling, GPA killer. Avoid.

the marking is unfair and he is never available for extra help. he tries to trick you on exams and instead of focusing on major concepts he asks you questions about tiny little details which aren't even important. he doesn't even scale the class. try to avoid him!

Absolute jerk. He is all nice and open like "come see me in office hours" but nearly impossible to get an appt. with. He was really rude in person and his test style was asking alot. Do yourself a favor...take with a different prof. There is a reason he's a sessional.

Only prof I've ever met that has an "exam hot-line" that goes until 11pm the night before the exam! It's pretty nice of him to sit in his office listening to our calls before every exam. Marking for assignments in particular are really specific and tough though. Questions were very welcomed and he was never condescending from what I experienced.

Lectures are worth attending as he's a great speaker. Notes are provided and studying from them alone got me a good grade. Assignment marking is quite strict and the exams are all MC which can become very tedious and tricky, but it's completely manageable! He was really nice to the students in his office when I went, so overall... good prof =)

Hey.. give him a little break. Of course the material was overwhelming and the exams were hard, it's not totally his fault. I just don't get why people say that the lectures were unrelated to the exam, because they are. I only studied his notes and got A. And, at least for me, I've never seen him showing disrespect toward students.

The course has lots of material, but it's all in the textbook. I'm the lazy type and I attended 5 lectures max but I read the textbook word for word and still got a good grade. His lectures are boring and pointless so don't go if you're the type who can just sit and read. His exam formats suck (All MC and variations of MC)

i love biochem and wanted to major in it. But this prof ruined everything. I worked so hard in this class, but it was all useless. I was very broken with my final grade in this class. i repeated it and got the best grade. I don't prefer him. His exams are very hard and weird. he seems helpful but is not in reality. AVOID HIM!!!
A difficult class, and a professor who is helpful-yet somewhat unfair. The exams are difficult purely because of their format. Exams (although scantron) still take 2+ weeks to grade.

This is one prof where the majority of negative student comments reflect 100% truth. He refused to use a mic, he treats students like dirt in person. His exams are unfair as they are weirdly worded. Be warned: you may have an B-B+ avg going into the final and he will give you a D overall. RIDICULOUS. AVOID!

I think some of the reviewers on here are being a bit harsh. First of all, the course itself it's pretty content-heavy, so you can't expect to get everything handed to you on a silver platter. I didn't do very well on the first midterm, but improved on my second. It's important that you really read the notes that he gives you rather than the text.

Has issues with giving the bigger picture. If you do not read the textbook you will be lost. But he doesn't want you to read the text, so go figure... Unfair grading schemes for problem sets. Generally an unstable person with some sort of a split personality disorder.

The worst prof of all my undergrad classes. Yeah, he knows what he's talking about but I think he teaches so he can hear himself talk. Tests are setup so he can tell you how stupid you are in the review session - he's NOT helpful. I liked biochem before this class, Briscoe ruined it. AVOID! Wait another term for another prof, you will be thankful.

Absolutely horrendous professor. He clearly loves the material and is okay at explaining during lecture but he is a grade A jerk in person. His exams are ludicrous, and he has no qualms about failing students. He refuses to listen to students and is rude and condescending. He called a student "stupid" in office hours. How very helpful. AVOID.

Let's put it this way, this professor is as specific with his answers as a key is that "fits" into a particular lock and in THAT lock only. He has a low tolerance for flexibility in his marking scheme and YES, he emphasises more on details that do not in any way demonstrate the students' comprehension of the course content itself.
Seems helpful enough in lecture, but tests are horrible.  Uses extremely unclear wording and multiple choice questions are very tricky.  Avoid if you want a good mark!

AVOID. Sure he knows how to do teach the lecture. Tests are his downside. Briscoe focuses his testing on your ability to follow his bizarre testing techniques with less emphasis on your comprehension of the material. Never given the full time for midterms. TA's don't help, they always respond "the prof said were not allowed to answer that".

Terrible prof, knows his stuff but shows no sympathy to students. Easy to get A or B on midterm if you work hard. But after the final everyone went down to D!! B to a D. Didn't respond to complaints. Had to get MBB head involved. Take him... to drop your GPA =)
Horrible Professor! Midterms are not that bad but the final was horrible.  Refused to scale even though the highest mark in his class was a B+, No A's.
simply, just don't take his class. NO SCALE at all, so his class will drop your GPA significantly.
Great at teaching. Makes class interesting even though the material wasn't all the time.

Best professor I've had at SFU. LECTURES: Engaging, very interesting, excellent lecturing ability. EXAMS: very fair, answers are in the notes, marks off for spelling, but w/ good reason and warns you before hand anyway. So don't know why everyone is rating him so poorly (probably b/c they got bad marks). COURSE: very material dense, but interesting

great lecturer,i looked forward to attending lectures.very helpful and kind.his midterms and final were all fair.every single question was from the notes.if you want to do good on the midterms you have to know the notes in great detail because he loves going into details of things and make really tough multiple choice questions n put them on tests

Scott's lectures are engaging and fascinating. However, his ability to create fair examinations and problem sets is the worst I've seen in my undergraduate career. To approximate a normal distribution of marks, he gives questions that cover material undisclosed in lectures, subtracts marks for silly spelling errors, and is not understanding at all.

Fairly decent prof. Seems un-approachable at first but genuinely seems to enjoy helping out students. A bit geeky in class, but otherwise very knowledgable. Notes were good. Tests were fair but some questions and marking scheme were absurd (ie: marks off for minor spelling error!) Still, I'd take him again for MBB.

One of the best profs I've had, when I attended class at least, but his notes were good and his interest level in the material was high. And he encourages class participation, so I would recomend this prof for anyone thinking of taking MBB ;)
Not a very efficient lecturer.

I think she's an excellent prof!  MBB courses definitely take a lot of work but if you pay super close attention to her in class, you don't even have to read the textbook.  Just review your notes and you'll do fine.  Very friendly and also approachable.
She can be monotone at times but she is very nice and always asks for student feedback and responds accordingly.
